THE INITIAL TOUCHDOWN OCCURRED ON EAGLES LANDING DRIVE THEN MOVED NORTHEAST TO THE INTERSECTION OF JAMES ROAD AND LAGUNDOVIE ROAD. THE PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT 3/4 OF A MILE. AN ESTIMATED 24 PROPERTIES RECEIVED DAMAGE WITH ONE STRUCTURE SUSTAINING MAJOR DAMAGE. THE WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 65 TO 85 MPH...EF0.
NORTHEAST END OF OREGON. TREES DOWN. CAMPER TURNED OVER. GARAGE DAMAGE.

BARN DAMAGE. ROOF OFF HOUSE.
MULTIPLE POWERLINES DOWN. ROOF OF BARN BLOWN OFF.

WEAK TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 4 MILES SW OF PORT CLINTON IN BAY TWP. TORNADO CONTINUED ON GROUND TO THE NE FOR ABOUT A MILE AND A HALF BEFORE LIFTING. DAMAGE PATH AROUND 30 YARDS WIDE. DAMAGE CONSISTED OF MAINLY DOWNED TREES. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS LOST SECTIONS OF ROOFING. A COUPLE OF HOMES ALSO SUSTAINED VERY MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. MAX WINDS ESTIMATED AROUND 75 MPH.
A FEW TREES AND LARGE LIMBS KNOCKED DOWN.
FLOODING ON WEST 23RD, WEST 43RD AND GEORGIA AVE. THE WATER WAS UP TO CAR DOORS IN SOME SPOTS. ONE PERSON HAD TO ABANDON THEIR VEHICLE ON WEST 23RD.
SEVERAL ROADS WERE IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOODING.
FLOODING CLOSED CHAPIN ROAD AT MASON ROAD. FLOODING WAS ALSO REPORTED ON SECTION LINE 30 ROAD.
